channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Meta's Adam Mosseri predicts AI token spending limits for engineers in the near future
item.description: In a recent interview on Lenny’s Podcast, Instagram head Adam Mosseri suggested that Meta may need to implement caps on AI token spending per engineer within a year or two. He argued that as AI costs ...

item.title: Alexandra Residents Protest Over City Power's Electricity Delays and Infrastructure Concerns
item.description: Residents of Alexandra, South Africa, have staged a protest against City Power, accusing the utility of failing to deliver on a 2025 promise to electrify the Mahauzana Flats. The community, led by Mma...

item.title: England captain addresses squad unity ahead of World Cup semi-final against Argentina
item.description: England captain Harry Kane has rejected claims of internal discord within the team ahead of their World Cup semi-final clash against Argentina. Kane dismissed media speculation about tensions between ...

item.title: Military Enlistment Surges in Canada and the U.S. Amid Economic and Geopolitical Factors
item.description: Military recruitment in Canada and the U.S. has reached record levels, driven by a combination of economic challenges, administrative reforms, and geopolitical tensions. Canadian Armed Forces exceeded...

item.title: CDC Reports Over 1,600 Lab-Confirmed Cyclosporiasis Cases in U.S., Spreading Across 34 States
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has reported a significant surge in lab-confirmed cases of cyclosporiasis, a diarrhoea-causing intestinal parasite, with the count rising to 1,645 ...

item.title: Kehlani Undergoes Surgery to Address Chronic Breathing Problems
item.description: Kehlani, the American singer, recently underwent nasal surgery after doctors found her nasal passages were 98% blocked due to swollen turbinates, a deviated septum, and a clogged sinus. The procedure ...

item.title: Asha Dachi Appointed New Director General of Tanzania Broadcasting Corporation
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has appointed Asha Dachi Mbaruk as the new Director General of the Tanzania Broadcasting Corporation (TBC), marking a leadership change at the state-owned broadcaster. Th...

item.title: Bulacan court resumes operations after hoax bomb threat
item.description: A bomb threat disrupted operations at the Bulacan Regional Trial Court (RTC) in Malolos, prompting an evacuation and police investigation. The Provincial Prosecutor’s Office received the threat around...

item.title: Suspect in school official's murder arrested in Sulu
item.description: A suspect in the murder of Sonatria Gaspar, a school official from the Schools Division Office of Jolo, has been arrested in Parang, Sulu. The 22-year-old suspect, identified as Ning, was apprehended ...

item.title: Supreme Court justices testify on rising security threats amid congressional scrutiny
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett have testified before Congress, highlighting a significant increase in threats against the judiciary following the leak of the Dobbs decision t...

item.title: VAR's Role in Controversial Football Decisions
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has highlighted ongoing controversies surrounding Video Assistant Referee (VAR) technology, which was introduced to reduce errors in football decisions. Despite its intent to p...

item.title: France and Spain Set for World Cup Semi-Final Clash
item.description: France and Spain are set to battle in a high-stakes World Cup semi-final at the AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas. France, led by Kylian Mbappe, aim to reach their third consecutive World Cup final aft...

item.title: Tenri School Students Engage with Senators on National Issues
item.description: Students from Tenri School in Embu visited the Senate for an academic exchange weeks after their school's presidential election gained online attention. The visit was arranged by the school's administ...

item.title: Trump Claims Vandalism Damaged Reflecting Pool, But Observers Note Tire Tracks from Motorcade
item.description: President Donald Trump drained the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on Monday, claiming vandals had created a 300-yard gash in its lining. However, reporters and on-the-ground observers saw no such da...

item.title: Capcom's Dragon's Dogma 2 Achieves Stable 30fps on Switch 2 with RE Engine
item.description: Capcom's Dragon's Dogma 2, which faced significant performance issues on its initial release across multiple platforms, is set to launch on the Nintendo Switch 2 on October 9, 2026. Producer Naoto Oya...

item.title: Brandon, Man., Monitors Floodwaters After River Crests
item.description: Water levels in the Assiniboine River are beginning to decline in Brandon, Manitoba, following a peak earlier this week. According to a city release, levels have dropped by approximately three inches ...

item.title: Internal TMC Conflict: Rebel Faction Demands Abhishek's Removal to Reunite Party
item.description: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) is facing a severe internal crisis as a faction led by Ritabrata Banerjee demands the removal of Abhishek Banerjee, Mamata Banerjee's nephew, to reunite the party. Veteran...

item.title: AFL Tribunal Downgrades Collingwood Defender Billy Frampton's Suspension to Fine
item.description: Collingwood defender Billy Frampton has had his suspension for rough conduct reduced to a fine by the AFL tribunal, allowing him to play against Carlton. Initially sanctioned for a heavy tackle on Nor...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Returns Home After Self-Deportation, Shares Life Challenges and Regrets
item.description: Lilian, a Kenyan woman who self-deported from the US, returned home after years abroad, sharing her experiences of rebuilding her life in Kenya. She explained that her decision to come back was influe...

item.title: Scientific research into human hibernation for space exploration and medical applications
item.description: The article explores the scientific investigation into inducing a hibernation-like state in humans to address challenges of long-duration space travel. Researchers are examining how reducing metabolic...
